title: 顺水推舟
summary:
During a conversation, Lyn admits to holding a grudge against Rosalyn and others regarding "unjust wealth." David Hunter questions Lyn about her marriage to Rolly Claude, whom he calls an idiot, but Lyn defensively refuses to explain. David then recounts Rosalyn’s history, describing her as a "fool with luck." He explains that after a failed acting career and a marriage to an intellectual Nigerian official in Cape Town, Rosalyn received a life insurance payout following his death from fever. She later married Gordon Claude in New York, but he was killed by a bomb only two weeks later, leaving her with a large house, jewelry, and a substantial income. David reveals his own motive: an Irishman who fought for England and joined the Allied Commandos, he followed Rosalyn to London after hearing of her marriage and wealth.

Later, at breakfast in the English countryside, Rosalyn expresses an urgent desire to return to America, complaining about staying near the Claude family. David expresses his pleasure in seeing the Claude family suffer from jealousy and resentment, labeling them "little fleas." He claims the family hates them and suggests they might even try to poison her. While David insists they are staying in the countryside for her health and to avoid the trauma of the London bombings, Rosalyn expresses suspicion. She asks if he is staying because of a woman she encountered one night, a girl who served in the Women's Royal Naval Service.
